subject: Project Management
title: Flow Efficiency
description: A quick, practical guide to Flow Efficiency.

hook.text: A task that takes 2 hours of work but 10 days to deliver has a flow efficiency of just 2.5%.

buildup.text: Most of a task's life is spent waiting — in queues, reviews, approvals — not being worked on.

discovery.text: Flow efficiency measures active work time vs total time. Most teams score below 15%.

twist.text: Improving flow efficiency doesn't mean working harder. It means reducing wait time.

climax.text: Kanban's goal is smooth flow: items moving steadily from start to done, with minimal waiting.

punchline.text: Speed up the waiting, not the working.

quiz.question: What does flow efficiency measure?
options: Active work time versus total time including waits
The number of features delivered per sprint
